free_text: Nuclear charge distribution in the region of asymmetric
fission of U-238 by protons of energy 20-85 MeV.
The target foils consisted of uranium foils of
natural composition (99.29% U-238). The thicknesses
used depended on the half-lives of the nuclides being
investigated 134 mg/cm2 for half-lives greater than
1 hour and 54 mg/cm2 for shorter half-lives.
The results have been discussed in terms of a
growing contribution from direct interaction
mechanisms.
Total number of neutrons emitted in the asymmetric
fission of U-238.
30 cm3 Ge(Li)
3. x 3. inch NaI(Tl) scintillation crystal
for monitor.
Stack assembly contains 5 foils. The target foil
was sandwiched between two similar foils to compensate
the recoil losses within the master foil.
The copper foil was placed upstream in the target stack
with respect to the direction of the proton beam and
separated from the assembly by a thin aluminum foil to
protect the copper foil from fission product
contamination.
Duration of the irradiation varied from
5 to 30 minutes with a beam current of the order of
1 uA.
The activity of each nuclide was followed for
several half-lives.
The average value was adopted throughout the
bombardment range.
Tbl.5-7 from J.Inorg.Nucl.Chem.,39(1977)1497
- By Compiler - Very many references, which were used
to compare experimental data are entangled.

Method of analysis is not clear from the article.
Cross sections were generated by determining the ratio
Pm-149(cum)/Nd-149(cum) in each sample produced in this
series of measurements. A value of 1.304 was found at
20 MeV, which was then used as a normalization factor
for all the others at higher energies. This is based on
the reasonable assumption that at 20 MeV almost all of
the yield of the 149 chain is distributed over Nd-149
and its precursors, except for a 0.04 mb contribution
from Pm-149, as determined from charge dispersion
curves constructed with other independent yields.
The estimated independent yields of Pm-149 have been
used only as a guide for definition of the charge
dispersion curves with an assigned 50% error.
No information about source of uncertainty

The independent cross sections of Pm-148-m have been
corrected for ground-state contribution using the
results of Umezawa (see REL-REF) at thermal energy (10%
correction was applied to increase the fractional
independent yield value).
Umezawa has found sig-m/(sig-m + sig-g) = 0.88 in the
thermal fission of 233U. It is reasonable to expect
this ratio to increase to close to unity at medium
energies since the production of the high-spin
(6-) Pm-148-m should be favoured over the low-spin
2333U(nth,f)148Pm isomeric ratio
Value of the uncertainty contains misprint,
probably.
